006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

010 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.nio.charset.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
027
028/**
029 * A fluent accessor for an HTTP response body returned by {@link RestResponse}.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Provides convenient methods for reading the body as a string, byte array, or stream.
033 * The body is read lazily; each method reads and returns the body content once.
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * Obtain instances via {@link RestResponse#body()}.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
040 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
041 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
042 * (and possibly earlier).
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
045 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * @since 9.2.1
049 */
050@SuppressWarnings({
051   "resource" // Eclipse resource analysis: response is borrowed; caller closes it after reading body
052})
053public final class ResponseBody {
054
055   private final RestResponse response;
056
057   ResponseBody(RestResponse response) {
058      this.response = response;
059   }
060
061   /**
062    * Returns the response body as a UTF-8 string.
063    *
064    * @return The body string, or <jk>null</jk> if the response has no body.
065    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the body.
066    */
067   public String asString() throws IOException {
068      return response.getBodyAsString();
069   }
070
071   /**
072    * Returns the response body decoded with the given charset.
073    *
074    * @param charset The character set to use for decoding. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
075    * @return The body string, or <jk>null</jk> if the response has no body.
076    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the body.
077    */
078   public String asString(Charset charset) throws IOException {
079      var stream = response.getBodyStream();
080      if (stream == null)
081         return null;
082      return new String(stream.readAllBytes(), charset);
083   }
084
085   /**
086    * Returns the response body as a byte array.
087    *
088    * @return The body bytes, or <jk>null</jk> if the response has no body.
089    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the body.
090    */
091   @SuppressWarnings({
092      "java:S1168" // 'null' is a documented sentinel distinguishing 'no response body' (e.g. HTTP 204) from an empty body; callers rely on it (see RestClientFeatures_Test.l04_responseBody_nullBody).
093   })
094   public byte[] asBytes() throws IOException {
095      var stream = response.getBodyStream();
096      if (stream == null)
097         return null;
098      return stream.readAllBytes();
099   }
100
101   /**
102    * Returns the raw response body stream.
103    *
104    * <p>
105    * Callers should not close this stream directly; close the parent {@link RestResponse} instead.
106    *
107    * @return The body stream, or <jk>null</jk> if the response has no body.
108    */
109   public InputStream asStream() {
110      return response.getBodyStream();
111   }
112
113   /**
114    * Reads the response body into a byte array and returns it, or returns {@code null} if there is no body.
115    *
116    * <p>
117    * Equivalent to {@link #asBytes()}.
118    *
119    * @return The body bytes, or <jk>null</jk> if the response has no body.
120    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the body.
121    */
122   public byte[] readAllBytes() throws IOException {
123      return asBytes();
124   }
125
126   /**
127    * Opens a token/record-streaming cursor over the response body using the parser negotiated from the response
128    * {@code Content-Type}.
129    *
130    * <p>
131    * Equivalent to {@link #asCursor(Parser, Class) asCursor(negotiatedParser, type)} where the parser is selected
132    * from the response {@code Content-Type} header; when no registered parser matches and no default parser is
133    * configured, a <c>415 Unsupported Media Type</c> is thrown.  Use {@link #asCursor(Parser, Class)} to read with an
134    * explicit parser.
135    *
136    * @param <T> The cursor type ({@link RecordReader}, {@link TokenReader}, or a concrete subtype).
137    * @param type The declared cursor type. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
138    * @return A cursor over the live response body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
139    * @throws IOException If the body is missing, the parser does not support the requested cursor surface, or the
140    *    produced cursor is not assignable to {@code type}.
141    */
142   public <T> T asCursor(Class<T> type) throws IOException {
143      return asCursor(negotiatedParser(), type);
144   }
145
146   /**
147    * Parses the response body to {@code type} using the parser negotiated from the response {@code Content-Type}.
148    *
149    * <p>
150    * When the header is absent or matches no registered parser and no default parser is configured, a
151    * <c>415 Unsupported Media Type</c> is thrown.  Use {@link #as(Parser, Class)} to force a specific parser,
152    * bypassing content negotiation.
153    *
154    * @param <T> The type to parse to.
155    * @param type The type to parse to. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
156    * @return The parsed body, or <jk>null</jk> if the response has no body.
157    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the body or the body could not be parsed.
158    */
159   public <T> T as(Class<T> type) throws IOException {
160      return as(negotiatedParser(), type);
161   }
162
163   /**
164    * Parses the response body to {@code type} using the given parser.
165    *
166    * <p>
167    * This forces the supplied parser, bypassing the {@code Content-Type} negotiation performed by {@link #as(Class)}.
168    *
169    * <p>
170    * A parse failure is surfaced strictly as an {@link IOException} wrapping the underlying {@link ParseException};
171    * the malformed body is never returned to the caller.
172    *
173    * @param <T> The type to parse to.
174    * @param parser The parser to use. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
175    * @param type The type to parse to. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
176    * @return The parsed body, or <jk>null</jk> if the response has no body.
177    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the body or the body could not be parsed.
178    */
179   public <T> T as(Parser parser, Class<T> type) throws IOException {
180      assertArgNotNull("parser", parser);
181      assertArgNotNull("type", type);
182      var body = response.getBodyAsString();
183      if (body == null)
184         return null;
185      try {
186         return parser.parse(body, type);
187      } catch (ParseException e) {
188         throw new IOException(e);
189      }
190   }
191
192   private Parser negotiatedParser() {
193      var h = response.getFirstHeader("Content-Type");
194      var ct = h == null ? null : h.value();
195      return response.getClient().getMatchingParser(ct).orElseThrow(() -> new UnsupportedMediaType(
196         "No parser matched the response Content-Type ''{0}'' and no default parser is configured on the client.", ct));
197   }
198
199   /**
200    * Opens a token/record-streaming cursor over the response body using the given parser.
201    *
202    * <p>
203    * This forces the supplied parser, bypassing the {@code Content-Type} negotiation performed by
204    * {@link #asCursor(Class)}.
205    *
206    * <p>
207    * The cursor reads directly from the live response stream &mdash; the body is not buffered into memory.  The
208    * caller owns the returned cursor and the parent {@link RestResponse}; close them when done.
209    *
210    * <p>
211    * When {@code type} is (or extends) {@link TokenReader} the parser must implement {@link TokenReadable};
212    * otherwise it must implement {@link RecordReadable}.
213    *
214    * @param <T> The cursor type ({@link RecordReader}, {@link TokenReader}, or a concrete subtype).
215    * @param parser The parser that opens the cursor. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
216    * @param type The declared cursor type. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
217    * @return A cursor over the live response body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
218    * @throws IOException If the body is missing, the parser does not support the requested cursor surface, or the
219    *    produced cursor is not assignable to {@code type}.
220    */
221   @SuppressWarnings({
222      "unchecked", // The produced cursor is verified assignable to 'type' before the cast.
223      "resource"   // The cursor reads from the borrowed response stream; the caller closes the cursor / RestResponse.
224   })
225   public <T> T asCursor(Parser parser, Class<T> type) throws IOException {
226      assertArgNotNull("parser", parser);
227      assertArgNotNull("type", type);
228
229      var isToken = TokenReader.class.isAssignableFrom(type);
230      var supported = isToken ? parser instanceof TokenReadable : parser instanceof RecordReadable;
231      if (! supported)
232         throw new IOException("Parser '" + parser.getClass().getName() + "' does not support the "
233            + (isToken ? "token-reader" : "record-reader") + " surface.");
234
235      var stream = response.getBodyStream();
236      if (stream == null)
237         throw new IOException("Response has no body to open a cursor over.");
238
239      Object input = parser.isReaderParser() ? new InputStreamReader(stream, StandardCharsets.UTF_8) : stream;
240      var cursor = isToken
241         ? ((TokenReadable) parser).parseTokens(input)
242         : ((RecordReadable) parser).parseRecords(input);
243
244      if (! type.isInstance(cursor))
245         throw new IOException("Parser '" + parser.getClass().getName() + "' produced cursor type '"
246            + (cursor == null ? "null" : cursor.getClass().getName())
247            + "' which is not assignable to the declared type '" + type.getName() + "'.");
248
249      return (T) cursor;
250   }
251}
